subject: Anthropology
title: The Social Body
description: A fast breakdown of The Social Body for builders.

hook.text: A thin body means discipline in New York but poverty in rural Nigeria. The body speaks a social language.

buildup.text: The body is not just biological. It's a canvas on which culture writes its values, norms, and power relations.

discovery.text: Mary Douglas showed that the body is a symbol of society. How we treat bodies reflects how we organize social life.

twist.text: Body ideals serve power. Standards of beauty, fitness, and normality discipline people into conformity.

climax.text: The body is never just personal. It's always social — shaped by culture, judged by norms, and disciplined by power.

punchline.text: Every body tells a social story.

quiz.question: What did Mary Douglas argue about the body?
options: The body is a symbol of society
